canonical_title: One-off request to reformat a pasted video transcript verbatim
description: User states they will paste video-transcript sections by timestamp and wants them returned 'as it is'. The assistant returns the transcript. The transcript narrates a third party building an AI news-digest app; that is the material being formatted, not the user's project.
why_it_matters: Benchmark guard: rich project-shaped content inside a transcript must not become a user project.
confidence_rationale: User's own words frame this as verbatim formatting; the project content lives inside the pasted/returned transcript.
uncertainties: Whether the user is the transcript's speaker or merely formatting someone else's video
inclusion_rationale: Kept as non-project interaction per hr-001.

canonical_title: Third-party AI news-digest build narration (transcript source material)
description: The returned transcript describes building a YouTube/AI news digest pipeline (digest agent, curator, email agent). It is source material being reformatted.
why_it_matters: Must be classified as transcript material so it is not mistaken for the user's project.
confidence_rationale: Delivered as a transcript inside a formatting task; no user ownership statements attach.
inclusion_rationale: Recorded as transcript_fragment (non-project).

canonical_title: Pesticide groundwater exposure & risk-assessment coursework (Exercises E_1/E_2/E_3)
description: A graded academic assignment computing dietary/water exposure, Hazard Quotients, ADI %coverage, Margins of Exposure, and a probabilistic intake assessment across population groups, driven by the user via Claude-in-Excel.
why_it_matters: A real owned coursework deliverable; the canonical false-project benchmark that should resolve to assignment.
confidence_rationale: Strong explicit evidence (matriculation number, structured exercises, expected-answer sections) and human resolution hr-002.
uncertainties: Course/institution name not stated in bundle
contradictions: Earlier selection rounds had labelled this conversation false_project
inclusion_rationale: hr-002 confirms academic assignment; meets six project signals but is better typed as assignment.

canonical_title: Workflow: relay prompts to Claude-in-Excel, paste results back to verify
description: User sets up and repeatedly enforces a relay pattern where the chat assistant writes prompts, the user runs them in Claude-in-Excel, then pastes results back.
why_it_matters: A genuine adopted workflow defining how the assignment gets done.
confidence_rationale: Explicit, repeated user setup ('you won't be driving you will be giving instructions to claude in excel').
inclusion_rationale: Adopted/repeated workflow, not a tool trace.

canonical_title: Claude in Excel (tool the user is learning/adopting)
description: Claude's Excel integration, which the user is new to and actively learning to complete the exercises.
why_it_matters: Context entity meaningful because the user is adopting it.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly states they are new to Claude in Excel and wants to advance with it.
inclusion_rationale: Adoption elevates the tool to a context entity.

canonical_title: Preference: write the Expected-answer sections in the user's own tone
description: User asks to rewrite the Expected-answer content 'using my tone'.
why_it_matters: A writing preference that recurs across the corpus (also mc-06).
confidence_rationale: Explicit here; cross-conversation stability is an inference.
inclusion_rationale: User-authored preference; linked to mc-06 voice preference.

canonical_title: Constraint: advance step-by-step without skipping steps (beginner)
description: User requires careful incremental progress 'while not jumping steps' and later tells the Excel agent to 'move incrementally'.
why_it_matters: Governs how help should be delivered.
confidence_rationale: Explicit in this conversation; generalization uncertain.
inclusion_rationale: User-authored constraint.

canonical_title: Self-study system for catching up on online lectures (beginner, deep understanding)
description: User builds a two-prompt study system to turn lecture transcripts into beginner-friendly teaching, with a goal that evolves from exam-prep to deep career-relevant understanding.
why_it_matters: A real owned learning initiative; one of several distinct activities in this conversation.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit requests, an artifact (the prompts), explicit goal change.
inclusion_rationale: hr-003: do not merge; this is one of the distinct activities.

canonical_title: Operating Systems lab (process management & scheduling, Kali Linux)
description: User asks for step-by-step help to work through an OS lab (ps, top, kill, renice, cron) as a beginner.
why_it_matters: Distinct owned coursework, separate from the lecture-study system.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user statements naming OS labs, Kali Linux, specific tasks.
inclusion_rationale: Distinct assignment; under full project bar.

canonical_title: Amazon SDE internship application — resume tailoring
description: Sophomore CS student tailoring a resume to an Amazon SDE internship with skills/requirements breakdown and ATS optimization.
why_it_matters: A real owned job-application activity with a deliverable.
confidence_rationale: Explicit user details: Amazon, SDE, sophomore CS, existing resume.
inclusion_rationale: Distinct job application.

canonical_title: Linear algebra problem-solving & exam questions (step-mirroring prompt)
description: User builds a prompt to mirror example solution steps onto new linear-algebra problems, then pastes real quiz/exam questions (norms, symmetric/orthogonal matrices, quadratic forms).
why_it_matters: Distinct exam-prep activity with real academic stakes.
confidence_rationale: Explicit prompt-building plus multiple pasted exam-style questions.
inclusion_rationale: Distinct exam-preparation activity.

canonical_title: User context: sophomore Computer Science student (beginner, career-focused)
description: User identifies as a 2nd-semester sophomore CS student with no prior internship experience, studying OS and linear algebra, applying to internships.
why_it_matters: Stable user-profile context spanning multiple activities and (per hr-004) later conversations.
confidence_rationale: Multiple explicit self-descriptions; hr-004 confirms profile continuity.
inclusion_rationale: Cross-cutting user context (hr-004).

canonical_title: Personal applied-AI-engineering learning & career roadmap
description: Over multiple days the user co-builds a research-grounded roadmap to become job-ready as an applied AI engineer: evaluating courses/certs, running deep research (Track A market demand, then Track B resources), incorporating feedback, and producing an interactive HTML roadmap.
why_it_matters: The user's central self-directed career project; the active-project benchmark.

inclusion_rationale: Meets all nine criteria; hr-007 confirms active.

canonical_title: Decision: research market demand (Track A) before matching resources (Track B)
description: User agrees to prioritize Track A over Track B and to run the research questions together.
why_it_matters: A concrete user-endorsed sequencing decision driving the roadmap method.
confidence_rationale: User explicitly agrees ('Yes that does make sense... phase A is more important').
inclusion_rationale: User-authored, explicitly accepted decision.

canonical_title: Research agenda: 2026 AI-engineering market demand, interviews, startup vs enterprise
description: A set of research questions (most-demanded skills, what interviews test, startup vs enterprise, agentic-AI adoption, highest-leverage skills/projects/credentials) the user ran via deep research.

uncertainties: Deep-research results themselves are a missing source

canonical_title: Constraint: avoid spiraling/rabbit-holes; roadmap must be gap-free and competitive
description: User flags that deep research could spiral and asks to avoid it while requiring a competitive, gap-free roadmap.
why_it_matters: Guides how much research effort to invest.

canonical_title: Proposed direction: skip the 23-hour ExamPro GenAI course; build instead
description: The assistant recommends skipping the ExamPro course/cert in favor of shorter courses and projects. Per hr-005 this is an assistant proposal, not a user-confirmed decision.
why_it_matters: Shapes the roadmap but must remain a proposal until the user endorses it.
confidence_rationale: Recommendation is explicit (assistant); no explicit user acceptance preserved (hr-005).
uncertainties: No explicit user acceptance of skipping ExamPro is preserved
inclusion_rationale: Recorded as proposed_decision/assistant_origin per hr-005.

canonical_title: User context: CS junior/senior, DC-based, ~May 2027 graduation, prior CodePath work
description: User is a CS student graduating ~May 2027 in the DC area with prior CodePath modules, targeting AI engineering plus SWE/infra/data roles.

description: A multi-week Unity game (hex map, ships/cards, tech tree, save/load, combat-to-map loop, main menu) built from a professor's GDD/SDD via AI tools (Bezi, Unity AI, Claude Code), driven toward a submission deadline with professor coordination.

canonical_title: Professor-provided GDD and SDD (game/software design documents)

description: The AI agent the user primarily drives to plan/edit Unity code and scenes; prompts relayed and results pasted back.

canonical_title: Third-party reference: 'AI Engineer Roadmap (2026)' document (Dave Ebbelaar / Datalumina)
description: A saved project document, 'ai-engineer-2026.md', authored by an external practitioner (10+ yrs AI, runs Datalumina) describing an applied-AI-engineering roadmap. It is reference material the user kept, not the user's own roadmap artifact.
why_it_matters: Tests source-vs-artifact discipline and project-document linkage: the document is a SOURCE, and Stage 3R provides no verified conversation link.
confidence_rationale: Document content is explicitly third-party authored ('I have been working in the field of AI 10+ years... my own AI development company'); not user-authored.
uncertainties: Stage 3R verified_conversation_links is empty — no confirmed link to the mc-04 roadmap conversation
inclusion_rationale: Project-document case; classified as external source per provenance, not as the user's artifact.

canonical_title: Client Radar accessibility review & remediation (Accenture client deliverable)
description: An accessibility audit of the Client Radar 'Client Intel Pack' PowerPoint generator: 8 issues (4 critical) across reading order, alt text, and phantom bullets, with a ~5-day code remediation plan in two TypeScript files, and an Accenture-branded executive-summary deck.
why_it_matters: A real client-facing engagement with deliverables, an effort estimate, and an identifiable state.
confidence_rationale: Design-chat carries detailed user-authored audit findings, fix plan, and effort estimate; embedded project does not match the exported project (design-chat provenance noted).
uncertainties: Design-chat content is mostly labeled user_authored but reads like a co-authored report; exact authorship of the audit text is uncertain
Whether this is paid client work vs. an internal exercise
inclusion_rationale: Client deliverable; typed client_engagement. Ties to mc-09 (the repo session that produced findings).

canonical_title: Decision: present the accessibility summary as a 3-slide deck in Accenture house style
description: User asks to produce a 3-page HTML/slide executive summary using Accenture slide colors and design choices.

canonical_title: Accenture (employer/brand context for the Client Radar work)

canonical_title: Finding: Client Radar is a backend-only Azure Functions PPTX service with zero a11y code
description: The Claude Code read established the architecture (5 Azure Function triggers, Cosmos DB, pptxgenjs, Terraform Accenture CIO modules) and that there is no accessibility code anywhere; the user relays and works from this.

canonical_title: Blocker: Claude Code repeatedly prompts for permissions; cannot run unattended
